explanation: Shortening a URL generates a unique 6-character short code and returns the full short URL.
Resolving a short code returns the original URL for redirect.
Custom aliases allow users to specify memorable short codes.

input: shorten('https://example.com/promo', { customAlias: 'sale2024' }) when alias already exists
explanation: Duplicate custom aliases are rejected to maintain uniqueness.
Attempting to resolve a non-existent short code returns a 404 error.
Expired URLs are cleaned up and return an error on resolution attempt.

input: shorten same URL twice (without custom alias)
output: Same short code returned both times
explanation: Duplicate URL detection returns the existing short code instead of creating a new one.
Analytics endpoint returns click count, daily breakdown, and referrer information.

output: First call: cache miss (reads from store). Second call: cache hit (faster).
explanation: Resolved URLs are cached. Subsequent resolves are served from cache for better performance.
Only valid HTTP/HTTPS URLs are accepted. Invalid formats are rejected.
